ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Burmese htamin thoke (rice salad). FOODON_00002809 (rice dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Burmese rice salad. A000E (rice-based dishes) is used as the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Burmese rice salad dish. No suitable parent code identified without risk of fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Burmese chicken curry rice salad is approximately 350 g, typically presented as a single plate combining rice, chicken curry, and dressing ingredients.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: cooked white rice (USDA FoodData Central ID 168878), chicken curry (FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Use in East Asia, chicken curry entry), and Burmese salad dressing components (fish sauce, lime juice, sesame, chickpea powder) from USDA SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for htamin gyi kyar hin thoke exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 55% cooked white rice, 30% chicken curry (bone-free, with sauce), 15% dressing components (fish sauce, lime juice, dried shrimp, chickpea powder, sesame seeds, onion, chilli). Energy and macros are cross-checked against analogous Burmese rice salad dishes described in FAO/INFOODS SEA documentation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white rice and mixed rice-based dishes; analogous estimate based on cooked white rice GI (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for htamin gyi kyar hin thoke. GI estimated at 56 based on the dominant ingredient being cooked white rice (GI ~64-72 per University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by the presence of protein (chicken), fat (curry sauce, sesame), fibre (chickpea powder, vegetables), and acidic dressing (lime juice, fish sauce), which collectively lower the effective GI of the mixed dish. This is consistent with the GI of comparable mixed rice salad dishes (GI 50-60) documented in peer-reviewed literature. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 64.75) / 100 ≈ 36.3, rounded to 36.0 for a 350 g serving containing approximately 64.75 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and dried shrimp
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced fish sauce, as a standard 350 g serving provides approximately 1,470 mg sodium, representing 64–98% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 1,500–2,300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from white rice base, partially offset by protein and fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that a standard serving carries a glycemic load of approximately 36, and should consider reducing the rice portion or substituting with lower-GI grain alternatives to improve postprandial glucose control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from chicken curry
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption due to high sodium content (~1,470 mg per serving) and moderate saturated fat (~4.2 g per serving); the dish does provide beneficial unsaturated fats from sesame seeds and lean protein from chicken.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Htamin Gyi Kyar Hin Thoke (ထိမိဂွိကြက်ကရင်သုတ်) is a traditional Burmese rice salad dish featuring cooked white rice tossed with chicken curry and a complex hand-dressed mixture of aromatics and condiments. The dish is a variant of the broader Burmese thoke (salad) tradition, in which cooked starches or noodles are dressed tableside with fish sauce, lime juice, dried shrimp, roasted chickpea powder, sesame seeds, sliced onion, garlic, chilli, and turmeric. The chicken component is typically prepared as a mild to moderately spiced curry with onion, garlic, ginger, and coconut milk or oil. The resulting dish has a complex flavour profile combining savoury, sour, nutty, and mildly spicy notes, with a soft rice texture contrasted by the crunch of sesame and chickpea powder. It is commonly served as a one-dish meal at Burmese restaurants in Singapore, including at establishments such as Golden Cart Myanmar Restaurant. Nutritionally, a standard serving (~350 g) provides approximately 518 kcal, 28.7 g protein, 15.8 g fat, and 64.8 g carbohydrate. The dish is high in sodium due to fish sauce and dried shrimp, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is moderate (~36 per serving), relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free but contains fish and crustacean allergens.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for htamin gyi kyar hin thoke exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, USDA). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis of constituent ingredients using USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA data. GI is an analogous estimate based on white rice GI moderated by mixed-dish factors. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated; no direct approved-source entry exists for this dish. Values should be validated against laboratory analysis or a future approved food composition entry if one becomes available. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used (USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA do not provide reliable vitamin D values for this dish type). 3. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA source for the analogous dish components and is not reliably estimable from the available data. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero as the dish uses plant oils and unprocessed chicken with no industrial hydrogenation. 5. The food_name in the input JSON (ထိမိဂွိကြက်ကရင်သုတ်) is the Burmese-script native name; the canonical English food_name has been set to 'Htamin Gyi Kyar Hin Thoke (Burmese Chicken Curry Rice Salad)' per Veritas rules. 6. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed without restaurant certification; the dish may contain non-halal chicken depending on sourcing. 7. GI estimated at 56 (medium) based on white rice GI moderated by protein, fat, fibre, and acid components of the mixed dish, per peer-reviewed analogous-estimate methodology. 8. Multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable for this Burmese dish; the cuisine category does not require these languages per Veritas multilingual rules (Burmese cuisine requires romanized local name only). 9. The cuisine_tags in the input include 'Lor Mee', which appears to be a tagging error on the field survey platform; this dish is not a lor mee and the tag has been disregarded.
